NOW THEREFORE the Parties agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS

1.1 All capitalised terms which are used in this RCA and in the Schedules and Annexes but which are not otherwise defined herein shall have the same meaning as the definitions under clause 1 of the Grant T&Cs as annexed in Schedule 1. The relevant documents (including any annexures and attachments) relating to the Grant that all Parties are required to comply with, are annexed in Schedule 1, which may be updated and/or amended by Grantor from time to time ("Grant Documents"). Without prejudice to the above, in this RCA and in the Schedules unless defined elsewhere or the context requires otherwise:

"Cash Contribution" means actual monetary transfer from a Party to the other;

"Confidential Information" means any information or material disclosed by one Party to the other Party that is marked at the time of disclosure as confidential (or other words of similar import). Where the information is not tangible, it will be considered confidential if it is confirmed in writing as being confidential within seven (7) days of disclosure;

"Final Research Data" (or the final dataset) means a set of data that has reached the last stage of its lifecycle, where researchers have stopped making any further changes or adjustments to it;

"Inventive Contribution" means any intellectual contribution which brings about the creation of Intellectual Property, whether patentable or not, made or to be made by a Party for the Research in accordance with this RCA. For the avoidance of doubt, Inventive Contribution can include contributions such as technical guidance and expertise provided by any Party. Inventive Contribution shall be agreed between the Parties in accordance with clause 7.3;

"IP Application" means any patent application, division and all applications for registration of copyrights, designs, semi-conductor layout designs, trade names, trademarks, service marks, and any other IP registration relating to any Research IP;

"Know-how" means any method, technique, process, discovery, invention, innovation, unpatentable process, specification, recipe, formula, material, molecule, gene, protein, regulatory element, design, plan, documentation, drawing, data or other technical information which is secret, substantial and identified or at least identifiable, that is to say, described or is able to be described in a sufficiently comprehensive manner.

2.1 Except as stated in this RCA, no relationship of agency, joint venture or partnership shall
exist or be deemed to exist amongst the Parties.
2.2 Nothing in this RCA shall prevent a Party from carrying out its usual course of business.
3. RESPONSIBILITIES OF EACH PARTY IN THE RESEARCH
Collaboration on the Research
3.1 The Parties intend to collaborate and work together on the Research, which is as set forth
in Section 2 of Schedule 1.
3.2 The Parties agree to use their reasonable efforts to complete the Research in accordance
with the terms and conditions of this RCA.
3.3 The Parties recognise that the Research is research and/or innovative in nature and

3.3A The Research may be revised and amended by the Parties by written agreement, subject to the Parties’ compliance with the relevant requirements in the Grant Documents, which may provide that the Grantor’s prior written approval is required or that the Grantor shall be notified in writing as soon as possible or that application(s) for extension be submitted before a certain time.  

**Supervision / Coordination of the Research**  

3.4 The Research shall be managed by the Principal Investigator (“PI”) named in *Schedule 3*, who will be assisted by the Co-Principal Investigator(s) (“Co-PI(s)”) and Collaborators named in *Schedule 3*.  

3.5 Each Party shall inform the other of any change to the PI, Co-PI(s) or Collaborator(s) (as the case may be) no later than two (2) weeks before such change is due to take place.  

3.6 The Parties shall ensure that the PI, Co-PI(s) and Collaborator(s) shall meet at agreed intervals from time to time to review the implementation of the Research. Each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses related to such Research review meetings.  

3.7 The Parties shall ensure that the PI, Co-PI(s) and Collaborator(s) shall maintain records of the progress of the Research, including but not limited to all expenditure incurred for or in connection with the Research, and provide each other with such records as may be required from time to time.  

3.8 The Parties shall ensure that the Final Research Data is complete and legible, and allows traceability and validation of the Research findings should the need arise.  

3.9 Each Party shall be responsible for the acts and/or omissions of its employees, agents and/or sub-contractors that carry out any part of the Research.  

3.10 Each Party agrees that while its representatives are on the other Party’s premises, its representatives shall adhere, at all times, to all applicable rules, regulations, policies and procedures, including, but not limited to, those on health and safety, of the other Party. Each Party hereby confirms that to the best of its information, knowledge, and belief all its representatives who are engaged in the Research have the necessary skills, expertise and training, including on laboratory safety procedures, to perform their obligations.  

3.11 The Parties shall ensure that the PI, Co-PI(s) and Collaborator(s) carry out the Research in accordance with all applicable laws, regulations, codes and guidelines.  

3.12 The Parties are committed to maintaining the highest standards of research integrity and the responsible conduct of research as defined in the Singapore Statement on Research Integrity. The Parties agree to cooperate in investigation(s) which result from any accusations of research misconduct and malpractice arising from this research collaboration. The Parties shall report to the Grantor all incidents or allegations of any

4.4 Upon termination of this RCA, each Party shall not incur any further expenditure on the Research and, if applicable, shall return to the Grantor all monies that have not been expended under the Grant.

4.5 For the purposes of this clause, written notice of termination may only be effected by the signatories to this RCA or by persons of equivalent or higher authority.

5. DECLARATIONS BY PARTIES

5.1 Each Party is an organisation duly organised, existing and registered under the laws of their respective country of incorporation.

5.2 Each Party has full power and authority to execute and deliver this RCA, and to carry out the Research, and such obligations shall be valid and legally binding and enforceable against it.

6A. PUBLICITY

6A.1 Save as otherwise provided under clause 6A.4, no Party shall make or authorise the making of any announcement, publication or other disclosure concerning the existence or subject matter of this RCA unless the other Party has given its written consent to such announcement, publication or disclosure, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.

6A.2 Save as otherwise provided under clause 6A.4, the Party making the announcement, publication or disclosure shall consult with the other Party in advance as to the form, content and timing of such announcement, publication or disclosure, and/or provide a copy of the proposed announcement, publication or disclosure. If no objection is made by the other Party within one (1) month, the first-mentioned Party may proceed to publish the announcement, publication or disclosure.

6A.3 Save as otherwise provided under clause 6A.4, where a Party notifies the other of its objection on the basis that the announcement, publication or disclosure contains its Confidential Information or patentable material, the Parties shall work together to modify the objectionable portions so as to allow the announcement, publication or disclosure to proceed.

6A.4 The Parties agree that [REDACTED] may publicise the Research at [REDACTED] own platforms, provided that the publicity materials contain no Confidential Information or patentable material.

6B. PUBLICATION

6B.1 Any Party may publish and otherwise publicly disclose information it has gained in the course of the Research including but not limited to information relating to the Research IP ("the Publishing Party"), subject to the provisions in this clause.
